title: Dead of Winter: A Crossroads Game

component_identification.content: Colony board, location cards, survivor cards, crisis deck, crossroads cards, objective cards (main and secret), exposure die, dice for actions, zombie standees, food/waste markers, and item decks by location. Each player controls multiple survivors with special abilities and personal morale stakes.

core_game_loop.content: Each round has player turns followed by colony upkeep. On turns, players spend action dice to move, search, fight, barricade, clean waste, contribute to crisis, and use cards. Crossroads cards inject narrative events. Group must meet main objective while managing morale and possibly a hidden traitor agenda.

detailed_rules.content: Game setup picks a main objective and gives each player a secret objective; some sets include one traitor. At start of each turn, reveal and resolve a crossroads trigger if conditions are met, often forcing thematic choices with consequences. Action dice determine what each player can do that turn; many actions require matching die values or expend cards. Noise from searching/combat can attract zombies to locations. Exposure checks on risky actions may wound, bite, or kill survivors. End of round: resolve food demand at colony, add waste penalties, check crisis contributions (failure often reduces morale or adds threats), spawn zombies based on survivor counts and exposure tracks, then advance round marker. Morale reaching zero loses immediately for everyone except possible traitor outcomes. Betrayal suspicion is part of the design, but even non-traitors may act selfishly to satisfy personal objectives.

scoring_and_endgame.content: The group wins if the main objective is completed before morale or round timer runs out and colony does not collapse. A traitor (if present) wins instead by fulfilling traitor objective while avoiding exile consequences. In no-traitor games, all players can win together if both main and personal objectives are met.

beginner_strategy.content: Contribute to crisis early unless you are certain the table has it covered. Food and waste are silent killers; assign someone to logistics each round. Avoid unnecessary exposure rolls with valuable survivors. Share intent out loud so actions coordinate, but do not reveal all hand details if secrecy matters. Exiling can help or hurt—use it only with strong evidence and a plan for lost workforce.
